title: Understand: A Compromise with Evil: Slavery at the Constitutional Convention
description: Check your understanding of the compromises on the slavery at the Convention in this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Road to Reform, the Constitutional Convention, and the Battle for Ratification
A Compromise with Evil: Slavery at the Constitutional Convention
Understand: A Compromise with Evil: Slavery at the Constitutional Convention

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these was NOT one of the crucial compromises made during the Constitutional Convention?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The balance between large and small states
Whether to declare independence from Great Britain
How to elect the president
The future of slavery

assessment_items.question_content:
**When it came to drawing up congressional districts based on each state’s population, it was decided that enslaved persons would \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: be counted as whole persons
be counted as three-fifths of a person
only be counted in the northern states
not be counted at all

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about the Slave Trade Clause is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It included the words *slave* and *slavery*.
It banned the international slave trade immediately, but allowed for the continuation of slavery in America.
It prevented Congress from ending the international slave trade until 1808.
It abolished slavery in the northern states.

assessment_items.question_content:
**At the Constitutional Convention, how did the framers address the issue of slavery?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Abolished slavery throughout the United States
Refused to include protection for the "right to property in man" in the Constitution
Included protections for slaveholders in the Constitution
Created a timeline on how to eventually end the practice of slavery

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is NOT true about the Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It uses the word *slavery*.
It discusses the international slave trade.
It includes a Three-Fifths Clause.
It includes a Fugitive Slave Clause.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Because representation in the House of Representatives is based on each state’s population, a key question at the Convention was how to count the enslaved population of a state. Ultimately, the delegates decided to \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: compromise and count enslaved persons as three-fifths of a person
count enslaved persons as whole persons, as Southerners wanted
exclude enslaved persons from the count, as some anti-slavery delegates wanted
postpone the debate about representation until the next presidential election

assessment_items.question_content:
**By the time the U.S. was founded, even many slaveholders opposed the Atlantic slave trade. The issue led to heated debates at the Constitutional Convention, where the delegates ultimately agreed to \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: end the international slave trade immediately, even though South Carolina threatened to walk out of the Convention
compromise by allowing Congress to ban the international slave trade in 1808
allow the international slave trade to continue indefinitely, without congressional interference
permit only the wealthiest merchants to continue in the international slave trade

assessment_items.question_content:
**On the issue of equality, the original Constitution \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: said that all men are created equal
said nothing about whether people were born equal
went into great detail
contained the Equal Protection Clause

assessment_items.question_content:
**Delegates from southern states demanded that the framers of the Constitution agree to the Three-Fifths Compromise, which required which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Three-fifths of the enslaved population of a state would be counted toward a state's population.
Only three-fifths of slaveholding states would have votes in the House of Representatives until those states agreed to end the institution of slavery.
Three-fifths of the amendments proposed  as part of the Bill of Rights must come from ideas offered by the southern states.
Only three-fifths of states would be needed to approve amendments to the Constitution that related to slavery.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which delegate offered some of the most powerful anti-slavery arguments at the Constitutional Convention?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Gouverneur Morris
George Washington
James Madison
Thomas Jefferson

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which article of the Constitution includes the Fugitive Slave Clause?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Article I
Article II
Article III
Article IV

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did the delegates at the Constitutional Convention agree to the Three-Fifths Clause and the Slave Trade Clause?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Many delegates feared that certain southern states would refuse to support the Constitution without compromises over slavery.
The southern states threatened to go to war with the northern states if they didn’t compromise at the Constitutional Convention.
The delegates gave into the demands of nations with which the U.S. had treaties, like France and Great Britain.
The delegates wanted the Constitution to include a specific year that slavery as a practice would end in the United States.
